text: Code reviews are a key practice in software development. When developers examine each other's code before it is merged into the main project, they can catch bugs early, long before the software is released. This saves time and money. Beyond finding errors, code reviews also promote knowledge sharing. Team members learn different approaches and techniques from one another, and new members become familiar with the codebase faster. Ultimately, this process helps raise the overall quality of the team's work, as everyone develops a shared standard for clean, reliable code.
